name: Government & Public Sector

users.context: Processes citizen applications (benefits, permits, licences), verifies eligibility against documents and rules, determines outcomes, and records decisions. High caseload, queue-driven, accountable for fairness and due process.
users.expertise: Expert in program policy and eligibility rules; moderate IT fluency on legacy line-of-business systems.

users.context: Submits applications and documents, checks status, pays fees, and renews licences through a public portal. Extremely varied digital literacy, devices, languages, and abilities; may be in a stressful situation (benefits, immigration).
users.expertise: Novice and infrequent; assumes nothing. Must succeed on first attempt with plain language.

users.context: Manages official records retention, responds to public-records/FOIA requests, applies redactions and exemptions, and certifies disclosures.
users.expertise: Expert in records law, retention schedules, and exemption categories.

users.context: Conducts site inspections (permits, code, safety), captures evidence (photos, notes), and issues findings or citations. Works offline in the field, often on a tablet or phone.
users.expertise: Expert in codes and field procedure; needs offline capture and geo-tagging.

users.context: Reviews and approves caseworker determinations, handles appeals and exceptions, monitors SLA and backlog, ensures consistency and audit readiness.
users.expertise: Expert in policy and oversight; consumes queue/SLA dashboards and exception views.

users.context: Runs solicitations, evaluates bids, awards and administers contracts under public procurement law; maintains an auditable competitive trail.
users.expertise: Expert in procurement regulation and evaluation scoring.

workflows.name: Citizen application intake and eligibility determination
steps: Citizen submits application and supporting documents
Intake validation and routing to a queue
Apply eligibility rules and request missing information
Notify applicant and record decision with appeal rights

workflows.name: Public records / FOIA request handling
steps: Log request and acknowledge within statutory clock

workflows.name: Field inspection and citation
steps: Travel and capture findings offline (photos, geo, notes)

workflows.name: Public procurement / solicitation and award
steps: Evaluate against published criteria with multiple scorers

risks: Wrong eligibility determination wrongfully denying or granting a citizen benefit
Missed statutory deadline (FOIA clock, appeal window, benefit timeliness) creating legal liability
Improper disclosure of PII/sensitive data, or failure to redact exempt material
Inconsistent or biased decisions across caseworkers undermining equal treatment
Granting a citizen or staff member access beyond their authorisation
Records altered or deleted contrary to retention schedule, breaking the official record
Inaccessible portal excluding people with disabilities (legal non-compliance) or non-English speakers
Procurement trail that cannot withstand a bid protest or audit
Lost field inspection data due to no offline capture
Citizen abandons a confusing form, failing to receive entitled services
environment_constraints: Extremely diverse public users: assistive tech, low literacy, many languages, low-end devices, low bandwidth
Legacy mainframe/line-of-business systems behind modern front-ends
Strict statutory deadlines and due-process requirements
Field work offline with later synchronisation
Public scrutiny, transparency, and audit obligations
domain_patterns: Immutable decision audit trail with stated reasons
audit_compliance: Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility (legally mandated for public services)
GDPR / national privacy law and data-minimisation for PII
FOIA / public-records statutes and retention schedules
Procurement regulations (e.g., FAR) and competitive-bid auditability
FedRAMP / security accreditation for hosting where applicable
accessibility_concerns: Mandatory WCAG 2.1 AA / Section 508 conformance across public-facing services
Plain-language content at a low reading grade level
Full keyboard and screen-reader operability, no colour-only status
Resilience on low bandwidth and older/assistive devices
Clear error recovery and save-and-resume for long forms
anti_patterns: Jargon-heavy, agency-internal terminology shown to citizens
Long unsaveable forms that lose work on timeout
Colour-only or icon-only status with no text equivalent
Opaque denials without stated reasons or appeal path
Hiding the statutory deadline clock from staff
Hard-coding rules so policy changes require redeploys instead of being auditable/configurable
English-only flows in a multilingual jurisdiction
Allowing record deletion outside the retention/legal-hold framework
